title: tephrosia dasyphylla subsp. butayei
intro: Tephrosia dasyphylla subsp. butayei, also known as the Egyptian milk pea or Butayei pea, is a plant species within the Fabaceae family, characterized by its unique features and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, which have been attributed to its alkaloid and flavonoid content. The health benefits of Tephrosia dasyphylla subsp. butayei include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use in traditional medicine to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant may include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, often used in combination with other medicinal plants to enhance their therapeutic effects.
